title: pelargonium × tinctum
intro: Pelargonium × tinctum is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, resulting from the cross between Pelargonium tomentosum and possibly a member of the P. fasciculatum species group. The medicinal properties of Pelargonium × tinctum are attributed to its high concentration of rosmarinic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including colds, coughs, and digestive problems, offering relief from sore throats and respiratory infections due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium × tinctum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
